title: Rivian Q3 2024 Financial Results and Guidance
text: Rivian released its third quarter 2024 financial results showing challenges and strategic adjustments. The company delivered 10,018 vehicles in Q3 2024, representing a 27% decline from Q2's 13,790 units. This decline was due to a supplier shortage affecting copper windings on R1T, R1S, and electric delivery vans. The company slashed its annual production guidance from 57,000 to 47,000-49,000 vehicles for 2024. Third-quarter revenue was approximately $1 billion, representing the company's first revenue contraction since going public. Despite lower output, per-unit losses improved thanks to cost-reduction initiatives. Rivian reported a loss of approximately $0.96 per share in Q3, compared to $1.19 in the prior year. The company emphasized manufacturing upgrades at its Normal, Illinois facility. R1T and R1S production was impacted by supply constraints. The EDV business remained relatively steady. Management provided detailed guidance updates addressing investor concerns about production capacity and delivery timelines.
